description: Wrapper around react-hook-form's FormProvider plus helper components that wire label / description / error IDs automatically. FormLabel gets htmlFor pointing at the control; FormControl sets id, aria-describedby (linking description and/or message), and aria-invalid when the field has an error. FormMessage renders the validation error when present.

keyboard.action: Moves between form controls in document order.
Submits the form when inside a single-line input, or inserts a newline inside a textarea.

focus.notes: Each control manages its own focus. On validation failure, consumers typically focus the first invalid field; react-hook-form's setFocus API or shouldFocusError: true (the default) handles this.

announcements: Each control announces its label, current value, and (when invalid) the associated error message via aria-describedby.
FormMessage content is linked via aria-describedby, so it's read with the field, not as a separate live region.
screenReader.notes: FormMessage updates silently when the error text changes; screen readers only re-read it when the user re-focuses the field. For synchronous validation on blur, consider pairing with aria-live on a form-level summary for long forms.

contrast.text: Labels use foreground, descriptions use text-muted-foreground, messages use text-destructive; all meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Invalid state is conveyed via aria-invalid plus the FormMessage red text; consider adding an icon for low-vision users

motion.reducedMotion: No animations in the Form primitives themselves

consumerResponsibilities: Always wrap fields in a FormItem so FormLabel, FormControl, and FormMessage share the same generated IDs.
Provide a FormDescription for non-obvious fields so screen readers can explain the constraint via aria-describedby.
On submit failure, scroll to and focus the first invalid field; react-hook-form's default behavior only focuses, which may be off-screen.
Use zod (or your schema library) resolvers for client-side validation; the component only wires the UI, not the rules.
